meaning: adj.濡傜櫨绉戣緸鍏哥殑,鐧剧鍏ㄤ功寮忕殑
examples: The life of the modern world has been recorded on film in massive, encyclopaedic detail.
I didn't know you were so encyclopaedic.
But a text itself is not a linguistic or an encyclopaedic system.
He was said to have encyclopaedic knowledge of the railway world, after devoting his life to the subject.
His knowledge of world literature may not be deep, but it is encyclopaedic.
